title: Ability to import from mtputty
author: wfeddern
body_snippet:
<!-- Feature Request -->
<!-- If you file a feature request, please delete the bug section -->
I have a large number of ssh sessions defined in mtputty (including subfolders) that I would like to migrate to mRemoteNG. ( http://ttyplus.com/multi-tabbed-putty/ )

iterations.description: Feature request to import connections from MTPuTTY (multi-tabbed PuTTY) which stores sessions in XML format, and a user has already provided a working Python conversion script showing the mapping is straightforward

comments.author: sinicis
comments.snippet: would be super handy! any news about it?

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Our fork ...

notes:
AI triage: Feature request to import connections from MTPuTTY (multi-tabbed PuTTY) which stores sessions in XML format, and a user has already provided a working Python conversion script showing the mapping is straightforward
Approach: Create a new MTPuTTYImporter class that parses the MTPuTTY XML session file (which contains host, port, username, folder structure) and maps entries to mRemoteNG ConnectionInfo objects with SSH protocol, similar to existing importers like PuttyConnectionManagerImporter
